steps.title: Identify the exponential decay structure
steps.content: The beam starts with $800$ photons at the surface. Each $7$ millimeters cuts the number in half, so the decay factor per $7$ mm is $0.5$. The number of 7-mm intervals in $x$ millimeters is $\frac{x}{7}$.

steps.title: Use a Desmos table regression check
steps.content: The first few model points are $(0,800)$, $(7,400)$, and $(14,200)$. Enter them as lists and run $y_1\sim a b^{x_1/7}$. Desmos returns $a=800$ and $b=0.5$, so the model is $$I=800(0.5)^{x/7}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice B halves the beam every $1$ mm, not every $7$ mm. Choices A and C use bases greater than $1$, so they grow instead of decay. Choice D has initial value $800$, decay factor $0.5$, and exponent $x/7$, so it is correct.
